issues.description: Outline credits Leibniz for dy/dx and Lagrange for f'(x). The reference (Unit 2, CHA 2.2) lists the topic 'Defining the Derivative of a Function and Using Derivative Notation' but does NOT attribute the notations to Leibniz or Lagrange by name. Attributions are historically accurate but unsupported by this reference.
issues.fix: Either drop the historical names or add a citation note that the attribution is general mathematical history, not from the CB CED.

issues.description: Outline references 'OpenStax Calculus Vol 1 Ch 3.1–3.6' and 'Khan Academy AP Calculus AB — Derivatives 1' as external resources. The reference document does not list any external resources, so these cannot be verified against it.
issues.fix: These are plausible real resources; verify titles/chapter numbers independently. No change needed if confirmed accurate.

issues.description: Outline trap #3 ('Forgetting the negative sign in d/dx[cos x] = −sin x and in the co-function trig derivatives (cot, csc)') is more specific than anything in the reference's Unit 2 trap list. Reference lists 'Misapplying product/quotient rule with wrong sign conventions' — related but distinct (product/quotient signs vs. trig-derivative signs). Plausibly correct pedagogical addition, but not directly supported by the reference.
issues.fix: Either reframe trap #3 to align with the reference's 'wrong sign conventions' framing, or accept as a teacher-added specific trap that complements the CED list.

overall_comment: All 10 CB topic codes (CHA 2.1–2.3, FUN 2.4–2.10) and the module title match the reference exactly. Three minor unsupported additions: Leibniz/Lagrange attributions, external resource names, and one extra AP trap on trig sign errors — all plausibly correct general knowledge but not backed by the reference.
